title: carthamus caeruleus var. hispanicus
intro: Carthamus caeruleus var. hispanicus, commonly known as the Spanish safflower, is a plant species belonging to the sunflower family that is native to the Mediterranean region. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which are attributed to its high content of flavonoids and phenolic acids. The health benefits of this plant include reducing inflammation and improving cardiovascular health, as well as exhibiting antioxidant and antimicrobial activities. The herbal preparations of Spanish safflower include infusions, decoctions, and ointments, which can be used to treat various health conditions, including skin irritations and wounds.
